food_subgroup: Fruit-flavoured tea drinks with probiotic dairy

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for guava-flavoured Yakult tea drinks. FOODON_03301710 (tea beverage) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR (Tea-based beverages) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for guava-Yakult tea blends in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 (Tea drink) used as closest parent. No specific code for guava Yakult tea.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup (400 ml / 400 g), as typically served at bubble tea and specialty tea shops in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: Yakult Original (USDA FoodData Central NDB 167512), guava juice (USDA FoodData Central NDB 09139), green/black tea brewed (USDA FoodData Central NDB 14355/14278), and sugar syrup; weighted by typical recipe proportions.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source for Pink Guava Yakult Tea as a composite beverage. Nutrients estimated from weighted component analysis: approximately 65 ml Yakult Original, 100 ml pink guava juice/puree, 200 ml brewed green or black tea, and 35 ml sugar syrup per 400 ml serving. Per-100g values derived from this blend. All component data from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; guava juice GI ~48 (low); Yakult probiotic drink GI ~46 (low); blended fruit-tea beverage estimated GI ~48 as weighted average of components.
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pink Guava Yakult Tea. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on: guava juice GI ~48 (Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, peer-reviewed), Yakult probiotic drink GI ~46 (University of Sydney GI Database), and brewed tea GI effectively 1 (negligible carbohydrate). Weighted average of components yields GI ~48. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (48 × 35.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.9. gi_category set to low per ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~32 mg per 400 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Pink Guava Yakult Tea is low in sodium (~8 mg per 100 g; ~32 mg per serving) and poses minimal hypertension risk; however, individuals with hypertension should be mindful of added sugar content and request reduced-sugar versions when available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content from guava juice and added sugar syrup (~8.2 g sugar per 100 g; ~32.8 g per 400 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Pink Guava Yakult Tea with caution due to its moderate glycemic load (~16.9 per serving) driven by free sugars from guava juice and added syrup; requesting a reduced-sugar or no-sugar option is strongly advised to limit postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat and cholesterol; guava provides vitamin C and polyphenols with potential cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pink Guava Yakult Tea is very low in saturated fat (<0.1 g per 100 g) and cholesterol, and the guava component contributes vitamin C (~120 mg per serving) and polyphenolic antioxidants; it is generally suitable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease when consumed without excess added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pink Guava Yakult Green/Black Tea is a popular Singaporean specialty tea-shop beverage combining pink guava juice or puree, Yakult probiotic fermented milk drink, and a base of either green tea or black tea, typically sweetened with sugar syrup and served chilled over ice. The drink is characterised by a vibrant pink-to-coral colour, a sweet-tart tropical guava flavour, a mild tangy note from the Yakult probiotic culture, and a light tea finish. The texture is smooth and slightly viscous from the guava puree. It is commonly sold at Chinese-style bubble tea and specialty tea chains in Singapore, such as Happy Cup. Nutritionally, the beverage provides moderate carbohydrates (~35 g per 400 ml serving) primarily from free sugars in the guava juice and added syrup, a small amount of protein (~1.6 g) from the Yakult dairy component, and negligible fat. It contributes meaningful vitamin C (~120 mg per serving) from the guava, and the Yakult component provides Lactobacillus casei Shirota probiotic cultures and a small amount of calcium. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 48 (low GI), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~16.9) due to the volume of free sugars. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced-sugar versions to manage postprandial glucose. The beverage is low in sodium and saturated fat, making it broadly suitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management when sugar is controlled. It is vegetarian, halal-certified (Yakult Singapore is halal), and gluten-free, but not vegan due to the dairy-derived Yakult component.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Pink Guava Yakult Tea exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from weighted component analysis of Yakult Original, pink guava juice, brewed tea, and sugar syrup using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data. GI is estimated from analogous foods in peer-reviewed GI tables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated (not directly measured) as no approved-source entry exists for this composite beverage. All component data sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reliably reported across all components in the source database at this blend level. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 for the same reason. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported with sufficient precision for this blended beverage in the source data. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1 as these micronutrients are not reported with sufficient precision for this blended beverage in the available component data from USDA FoodData Central. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no trans fats are present in any of the component ingredients per USDA FoodData Central. 4. GI is estimated at 48 (low) based on analogous foods from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (peer-reviewed, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition) and the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (48 × 35.2 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 16.9. 5. The food_name uses 'Green/Black Tea' to reflect the menu notation 'G/B Tea' (green or black tea base option). 6. Halal status: Yakult Singapore Pte Ltd holds MUIS halal certification; the beverage is considered halal. 7. Price SGD 11.40 as recorded from field survey data (Happy Cup - Funan); price data preserved from input but not used as a nutritional source.
